title: Workflow Definition
description: JSON Schema for workflow-go process definitions. Defines the structure of a BPMN-like workflow with gateways, events, tasks, and sub-processes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the process definition

key.description: Business key for versioning; used with version field for deployment

startEventId.description: ID of the start event element; must reference an element with type 'startEvent'

elements.description: List of flow elements (nodes) in the process

sequenceFlows.description: List of sequence flows (edges) connecting elements

flowElement.description: Base type for all flow elements. The 'type' field determines which additional fields apply.

id.description: Element ID, unique within the process definition

name.description: Element display name; supports ${} variable templates

then.title: StartEvent
then.description: Process starting point. Must have exactly one outgoing sequence flow.

then.title: EndEvent
then.description: Process termination point. Consumes incoming token and triggers completion check.

then.title: UserTask
then.description: Human task requiring explicit completion via CompleteTask API. Supports dynamic assignee via ${} template.

assignee.description: User or ${expression} assigned to this task

then.title: ServiceTask
then.description: Auto-completing task. Completes immediately upon arrival.

then.title: ExclusiveGateway (XOR)
then.description: Routes to exactly one outgoing flow. Conditions evaluated in order; first match wins. DefaultFlowID is used when no condition matches.

defaultFlowId.description: Fallback sequence flow ID when no condition matches

then.title: ParallelGateway (AND)
then.description: Synchronizes (join) or splits (fork) execution paths. Join waits for ALL incoming tokens. Split activates ALL outgoing flows.

then.title: InclusiveGateway (OR)
then.description: Evaluates conditions on all outgoing flows; activates every flow whose condition is true. DefaultFlowID used when no condition matches.

then.title: BoundaryEvent
then.description: Event attached to a UserTask or ServiceTask. Triggers when timer fires or signal/message arrives. Only interrupting (CancelActivity=true) supported in v1.

attachedToRef.description: ID of the activity this event attaches to

cancelActivity.description: True = interrupting (consumes the activity's token). Default: true

then.title: IntermediateCatchEvent
then.description: Wait state in the main flow. Pauses execution until the specified event (timer/signal/message) occurs.

then.title: IntermediateThrowEvent
then.description: Fires a signal or message and continues immediately without waiting.

then.title: CallActivity (SubProcess)
then.description: Calls another process definition as a sub-process. Waits for the sub-process to complete before continuing.

calledElement.description: The 'key' of the process definition to call

inheritVariables.description: If true, copies all parent process variables into the child

sequenceFlow.description: A directed edge connecting two flow elements.

conditionExpression.description: Optional ${expression} condition for gateway-outgoing flows. Evaluated at runtime against process variables.

loopCharacteristics.description: Multi-instance configuration for a UserTask (会签). When set, generates multiple activity instances.

isSequential.description: True = sequential (one at a time), False = parallel (all at once)

loopCardinality.description: Fixed number of instances as a numeric expression

collection.description: Variable name of the input list/array

elementVariable.description: Variable name assigned per instance from the collection item

completionCondition.description: ${expression} for early termination; evaluated after each instance completes

timerEventDefinition.description: Defines a timer trigger. One of timerDuration, timerDate, or timerCycle is required.

signalEventDefinition.description: Defines a named signal trigger for cross-process coordination.

signalRef.description: Signal name; matched with ReceiveSignal calls

messageEventDefinition.description: Defines a named message trigger. In v1, messages use the same subscription mechanism as signals.
